bead.title: ddx home: add worktree registry CLI and focused tests
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The current bead mixes a focused worktree-registry behavior change with a repo-wide path-audit sweep. That blocks execution because the registry work is small and local, while the audit spans hundreds of call sites.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:166` and `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:224` already bootstrap and register the first worktree, but `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot_test.go:111` only proves the first-worktree master case. There is no operator command surface for `ddx project worktree master <path>`, so operators cannot re-pin `worktrees.json.master` even though the design requires it.

PROPOSED FIX
- `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go`: expose safe registry read/update helpers for `worktrees.json` and reuse the bootstrap lock for operator writes.
- `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot_test.go`: add `TestWorktreeRegistry_SubsequentRegisterNonMaster` covering a second checkout that registers without replacing master.
- `cli/cmd/project_worktree.go`: add `ddx project worktree list` and `ddx project worktree master <path>`.
- `cli/cmd/project_worktree_test.go`: add `TestWorktreeRegistry_OperatorRePin` that drives the CLI and inspects the rewritten registry.

NON-SCOPE
- Replacing hardcoded `.ddx` joins outside the worktree-registry command/test path.
- Wiring the repo-wide lefthook audit.

DEPS
- No deps.
bead.acceptance:
1. `TestWorktreeRegistry_SubsequentRegisterNonMaster` passes and proves a later bootstrap appends a second `paths[]` entry without changing `master`.
2. `TestWorktreeRegistry_OperatorRePin` passes and proves `ddx project worktree master <path>` rewrites `worktrees.json.master` to the operator-selected checkout.
3. `ddx project worktree list` and `ddx project worktree master` are wired into the root command tree under `project worktree`.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/ddxroot ./cmd/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
